example_prompt: Add a new Notch Pay beneficiary for MTN MoMo to use as a transfer recipient.

channel.description: Payment channel slug, e.g. "cm.mtn" for MTN Cameroon or "cm.orange" for Orange Money Cameroon.

account_number.description: Phone number or account number associated with the channel (e.g. +237XXXXXXXXX for mobile money).

email.description: Optional email address of the beneficiary.

phone.description: Optional phone number of the beneficiary.

description.description: Optional free-form description for the beneficiary, useful for internal labelling (e.g. "Payroll - Marketing team").

country.description: Optional ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g. "CM"). When omitted, Notch Pay infers the country from the channel slug.

reference.description: Optional unique reference you assign to this beneficiary for reconciliation. Notch Pay rejects duplicates.

id.description: Unique beneficiary ID. Store this to use in transfer and delete operations.

payment_method.description: The resolved payment method for this beneficiary.

gotchas: The Authorization header value is your public key with no prefix — do not add 'Bearer ' or 'Basic ' before it.
The channel slug must exactly match a Notch Pay supported channel (e.g. "cm.mtn", "cm.orange"). An invalid channel returns a 422 error without listing valid values.
account_number must be a valid phone number for mobile money channels — include the country code (e.g. +237XXXXXXXXX). Passing just a local number may fail silently or create an unusable beneficiary.
Store the returned beneficiary.id immediately — it is required to initiate transfers to this recipient and to delete the beneficiary later.
This endpoint requires your Notch Pay **private key** in the Authorization header, not the public key used for create_payment. Using a public key returns 406 Not Acceptable.
